_units: median wall-clock in milliseconds from pytest-benchmark default config (warmup absorbed)
_notes: Post-T30 baseline. T30 Step 2 (this commit) hoists the predicate-to-u8 + MLX cumsum work out of the per-column compaction loop in execute_filter_compact: with 3 surviving columns the cumsum FFI used to run 3x at ~48ms each (~144ms redundant work per filter); now it runs once and the (keep, prefix, n_out) triple is shared across columns. filter_simple dropped from 221.6ms to 117.2ms (47%), and every other Metal query improved correspondingly. The dominant remaining cost is the MLX cumsum FFI itself (CxxVector push/pop overhead) — T30 Step 3 owns that fix.
